skill.name: code-reviewer
skill.description: Review pull requests and project code for quality, maintainability, performance, and security concerns across common application stacks.
skill.summary: Structured code review guidance with checklist references and local report helpers.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the weak-crypto, reconnaissance, environment-file, and command-execution hits are documentation examples or placeholder text. The only executable behavior of note is optional JSON report writing to a user-supplied output path in the helper scripts, so the skill is low risk and safe to publish.

low_findings.title: User-Controlled Report Output Path
low_findings.description: The helper scripts write JSON output to the path supplied with the output option. This is expected report-generation behavior, but it can overwrite files accessible to the current user if invoked carelessly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct file writes are present at the cited lines and the path comes from a command-line argument. The behavior is transparent and local, with no evidence of malicious intent or hidden exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Alerts Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to fenced bash examples and usage text. They document how a user may run local tools and do not execute commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown code fences or inline usage references, not executable skill code. No dynamic shell invocation or Ruby backtick execution was found in these files.

low_findings.title: Weak-Crypto And Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography and low reconnaissance alerts map to descriptive prose, headings, and placeholder reference content. No hashing algorithm call, credential handling, system probing logic, or network behavior was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited files found generic text and argument definitions, not cryptographic primitives or reconnaissance code. The scanner labels do not match the semantic context.

low_findings.title: Environment File Access Alert Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The environment-file finding is a setup example that copies an example file to a local environment file. The skill does not read, parse, transmit, or expose environment secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is inside a Markdown setup command block. No script code accesses .env files or environment secrets.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Report Output Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper scripts write JSON output to the path supplied with the output option. This is expected report-generation behavior, but it can overwrite files accessible to the current user if invoked carelessly.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct file writes are present at the cited lines and the path comes from a command-line argument. The behavior is transparent and local, with no evidence of malicious intent or hidden exfiltration.

content.user_title: Review Code Quality Across Pull Requests
content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss security, maintainability, and consistency issues when teams rely on memory alone. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured review workflow with checklists, scripts, and report guidance.
actual_capabilities: Guides structured reviews for TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Swift, Kotlin, and Go projects.
Provides checklist references for quality, performance, security, and maintainability review topics.
Includes local Python helper scripts for PR analysis, code quality checks, and report generation.
Supports JSON output from helper scripts when users pass an output path.
Documents common development, analysis, and deployment commands for review context.
limitations: The helper scripts are template-level tools and do not perform deep static analysis by default.
Review quality depends on the files, diffs, and project context supplied by the user.
The skill does not replace language-specific security scanners or dependency audit tools.
Report output paths should be chosen carefully to avoid overwriting local files.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Review a feature branch for correctness, maintainability, testing gaps, and security concerns before merge.

use_cases.title: Team Review Standardization
use_cases.description: Create a repeatable review checklist that keeps feedback consistent across repositories and reviewers.

use_cases.title: Release Readiness Check
use_cases.description: Assess code quality, risk areas, and missing validation before a production release.

prompt_templates.title: Basic File Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changed files for correctness, readability, and obvious bugs. List findings by severity with file and line references.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-reviewer checklist to review this pull request. Focus on defects, missing tests, security risks, and maintainability concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Review code that handles data, authentication, or external input.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change for security issues. Check input validation, authorization, secret handling, unsafe file access, and dependency risk.

prompt_templates.title: Release Gate Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this release candidate using quality, performance, security, testing, and operational readiness criteria. Provide blockers and recommended fixes.

output_examples.input: A pull request that adds a new API endpoint and database query.
output: High: Missing authorization check before returning customer records.
Medium: Query path needs a test for empty results and invalid input.
Low: Rename a helper function so its side effects are clearer.

output_examples.input: A small refactor that changes error handling and logging.
output: Add one regression test for the new error branch.
Avoid logging request identifiers with user-provided values unless they are sanitized.

best_practices: Provide the actual diff, relevant tests, and project conventions before asking for review.
Ask for severity-ranked findings with file and line references.
Use the helper scripts on local paths and review generated output before sharing it.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad approval without providing the changed files or context.
Do not treat generated review comments as a replacement for tests and security scanners.
Do not write report output to important paths without checking whether the file already exists.

faq.question: Does this skill execute reviews automatically?
faq.answer: It provides review guidance and local helper scripts. The scripts run only when the user invokes them.

faq.question: Which languages does it cover?
faq.answer: The skill mentions TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Swift, Kotlin, and Go, with general guidance for common application stacks.

faq.question: Can it find security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It can guide security review and highlight suspicious patterns, but it should be paired with dedicated security tools.

faq.question: Does it send code to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access or external data transmission was found in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: The confirmed executable concern is local file writing to a user-provided report path. Other static alerts were documentation false positives.

faq.question: How should teams use it safely?
faq.answer: Run scripts only on intended project paths, choose output files carefully, and verify findings against the codebase.
